back_translation: The word of the LORD came to Jonah a second time, saying,
"Get up, go to the great city of Nineveh, and proclaim to that city the proclamation that I will tell you."
This time Jonah got up and went to Nineveh, according to the word of the LORD. Now Nineveh was an exceedingly great city — so great that crossing it required a three-day journey.
Jonah began to enter the city, having walked a one-day distance, and then he proclaimed: "Forty more days, and Nineveh will be overthrown."
Then the people of Nineveh believed God. They proclaimed a fast and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them to the least.
When the word reached the king of Nineveh, he rose from his throne, took off his royal robe, covered himself with sackcloth, and sat on a heap of ashes.
He had it proclaimed throughout Nineveh: "By the royal decree of the king and his nobles: Let no human or animal, herd or flock, taste anything. Do not eat food or drink water.
Let both human and animal be covered with sackcloth, and let the people cry out to God earnestly. Let everyone turn from his evil way, and from the violence that is in his hands.
Who knows? God may turn and change his mind; he may turn from his fierce anger, and we will not perish."
When God saw their actions — that they had turned from their evil way — God changed his mind concerning the calamity that he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.
